title: Does the eva community care more for theme over character
display_title: Does the eva community care more for theme over character
wikitext:
[]: Does the eva community care more for theme over character?             Quote Post by Sen » Sun Oct 29, 2017 4:56 am It's a common criticism I've heard of and I can't entirely disagree... Characters are treated as modes for Eva's themes rather than individuals with their own traumas, warped psychology and agency.

[]: Re: Does the eva community care more for theme over character?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun Oct 29, 2017 5:36 am But are not the individuals' traumas and resultant warped psychology the theme of Eva in any case? What other themes do you have in mind?

[]: Re: Does the eva community care more for theme over character?             Quote Post by Sen » Sun Oct 29, 2017 6:27 am pwhodges wrote: But are not the individuals' traumas and resultant warped psychology the theme of Eva in any case? What other themes do you have in mind?

[]: Re: Does the eva community care more for theme over character?             Quote Post by Asuka'sBigBrother » Sun Oct 29, 2017 11:26 pm When a show uses characters as a mode to convey thematic messages, that's a good thing. The characterization is so good in NGE partially because of how it all connects back to a central theme,
